components.name: Accordion
components.description: Collapsible content sections with controlled/uncontrolled modes and keyboard navigation.

props.name: panels
props.description: Array of panel data objects containing itemKey, title, content, and optional class overrides

props.name: highlightedKey
props.description: Key of the panel to highlight and scroll into view

props.name: highlightedClass
props.description: CSS class applied to highlighted panel header

props.name: isSimple
props.description: When true, renders without borders and background styling

props.name: gap
props.description: Gap between panels (Tailwind spacing value). When set, panels display as separate cards.

props.name: exclusive
props.description: When true, only one panel can be open at a time. Opening a panel closes any other open panel.

props.name: openPanels
props.description: Controlled state mapping panel keys to open/closed state

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Callback when panel state changes (required for controlled mode)

props.name: class
props.description: Additional CSS classes for the accordion container

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Simple accordion with default styling. Panels can be opened and closed independently.

examples.title: Styled Variant
examples.description: Set isSimple to false for bordered panels with background highlights.

examples.title: Separated Cards
examples.description: Set a gap to display each panel as a separate card. Useful for distinct content sections.

examples.title: Exclusive Mode
examples.description: Only one panel can be open at a time. Opening a panel automatically closes others.

examples.title: Controlled Mode
examples.description: Manage open/closed state externally for full control over panel behavior.

examples.title: Highlighted Panel
examples.description: Highlight a specific panel and scroll it into view. Useful for search or navigation.

examples.title: Custom Panel Styling
examples.description: Each panel can have custom classes for the container, title, and content areas.

examples.title: Color Variants
examples.description: Five color variants for different message types.

examples.title: With Icon
examples.description: Display an icon alongside the alert message.

examples.title: With Additional Content
examples.description: Add extra content below the main message.

components.name: BarChart
components.description: D3-based bar chart with composable axes, grid, tooltips, and support for grouped bars.

props.name: width
props.description: Base width of the chart in pixels (required)

props.name: height
props.description: Base height of the chart in pixels (required)

props.name: data
props.description: Array of data objects to plot (required)

props.name: barGap
props.description: Gap between bars within a group (0-1 ratio of bar width)

props.name: barCategoryGap
props.description: Gap between bar groups (0-1 ratio of category width)

props.name: rwidth
props.description: Responsive width override (from ResponsiveContainer)

props.name: rheight
props.description: Responsive height override (from ResponsiveContainer)

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Accessible label for screen readers

props.name: title
props.description: SVG title element for accessibility

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.description: Navigation trail showing page hierarchy with support for custom link components.

props.name: children
props.description: Breadcrumb.Item elements

props.name: class
props.description: Additional CSS classes for the nav element

examples.title: Basic Breadcrumb
examples.description: Simple breadcrumb navigation with links.

examples.title: With Current Page
examples.description: Using isCurrent prop to mark the current page.

examples.title: All Links
examples.description: Breadcrumb where all items are clickable.

components.usage:
import { Breadcrumb } from '@kayou/ui';

        // Basic usage
        <Breadcrumb>
          <Breadcrumb.Item href="/">Home</Breadcrumb.Item>
          <Breadcrumb.Item href="/products">Products</Breadcrumb.Item>
          <Breadcrumb.Item>Details</Breadcrumb.Item>
        </Breadcrumb>

        // With custom link component (e.g., SolidJS Router)
        import { Link } from '@solidjs/router';

        <Breadcrumb>
          <Breadcrumb.Item href="/" as={Link}>Home</Breadcrumb.Item>
          <Breadcrumb.Item href="/about" as={Link}>About</Breadcrumb.Item>
        </Breadcrumb>

        // Marking current page
        <Breadcrumb>
          <Breadcrumb.Item href="/">Home</Breadcrumb.Item>
          <Breadcrumb.Item isCurrent>Current</Breadcrumb.Item>
        </Breadcrumb>

components.name: DataTable
components.description: Table with virtualization, pagination, row selection, and a comprehensive filter system.

props.name: data
props.description: Array of data rows to display (required)

props.name: columns
props.description: Column configurations with key, label, width (percentage), optional render function and tooltip

props.name: loading
props.description: Whether the table is in a loading state (shows skeleton)

props.name: error
props.description: Error state for the table

props.name: errorMessage
props.description: Message to display when error is truthy

props.name: noDataMessage
props.description: Message to display when data array is empty

props.name: seeMoreText
props.description: Text for the expandable

props.name: filterButtonText
props.description: Text for the filter button that opens the popover

props.name: elementsPerPageText
props.description: Text label for the per-page control

props.name: selectedElementsText
props.description: Function to format the selected elements text

props.name: validating
props.description: Shows a loading overlay while refreshing data

props.name: rowSelection
props.description: Whether row selection checkboxes are enabled

props.name: searchBar
props.description: Whether to show the search bar

props.name: configureColumns
props.description: Whether to allow users to toggle column visibility

props.name: expandable
props.description: Whether the table can expand to full-view mode when data exceeds defaultRowsCount

props.name: defaultRowsCount
props.description: Number of rows to show before the

props.name: defaultColumns
props.description: Array of column keys to show by default

props.name: pageTotal
props.description: Total number of pages for pagination

props.name: onPageChange
props.description: Callback fired when page changes

props.name: perPageControl
props.description: Whether to show the per-page control dropdown

props.name: rowHeight
props.description: Fixed row height in pixels for virtualization (use with expandable)

props.name: estimatedRowHeight
props.description: Estimated row height for dynamic virtualization when row heights vary

props.name: filterConfigs
props.description: Filter configurations for the built-in filter system. Each config specifies key, label, fieldType, dataType, and optional operators/options.

props.name: filterState
props.description: Controlled filter state (Map of key to ActiveFilter)

props.name: onFiltersChange
props.description: Callback when filters change (for controlled mode)

props.name: filterMode
props.description: Internal applies filters client-side. External only tracks state for server-side filtering.

props.name: addFilterText
props.description: Text for the add filter link in the filter popover

props.name: resetText
props.description: Text for the reset button in the filter popover

props.name: applyText
props.description: Text for the apply/submit button in the filter popover

props.name: noFiltersText
props.description: Text shown when no filters have been added yet

props.name: filters
props.description: Custom filter components (legacy, use filterConfigs instead)

props.name: activeFilterCount
props.description: Number of active filters to display (for custom filters)

props.name: footer
props.description: Whether to show the footer with pagination controls

components.name: DatePicker
components.description: Calendar input supporting single, multiple, and range selection with locale-aware formatting. Requires DatePickerProvider.

props.name: type
props.description: Selection mode: single date, multiple dates, or date range.

props.name: locale
props.description: Locale identifier for date formatting and day/month names (e.g.,

props.name: value
props.description: Current date value(s). Object with date, startDate/endDate, or multipleDates depending on type.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Callback fired when the date selection changes.

props.name: displayFormat
props.description: Display format for dates in the input field.

props.name: minDate
props.description: Minimum selectable date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).

props.name: maxDate
props.description: Maximum selectable date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).

props.name: label
props.description: Label displayed above the input.

props.name: helperText

props.name: isLoading

props.name: popoverPosition

props.name: showTime

props.name: minuteStep
props.description: Minute step increment for the time picker.

props.name: secondStep
props.description: Second step increment for the time picker.

props.name: showShortcuts
props.description: Show shortcuts panel on the left side of the calendar.

props.name: shortcuts
props.description: Custom shortcuts for this instance. Overrides provider shortcuts.

props.name: weekStartsOn

components.name: DynamicVirtualList
components.description: Virtualized list supporting variable row heights with automatic measurement and caching.

props.name: items
props.description: Reactive accessor returning the array of items to render. Must be a function/accessor, not a raw array.

props.name: rootHeight
props.description: Maximum visible height of the list container in pixels. Determines the viewport size.

props.name: estimatedRowHeight
props.description: Initial estimate for row height in pixels. Used before actual measurements are available. Should be close to average expected height for best initial render.

props.name: children
props.description: Render function for each item. Receives the item and a reactive index accessor. Content can have any height.

props.name: overscanCount
props.description: Number of extra items to render above and below the visible area. Helps prevent flickering during scrolling.

props.name: containerWidth
props.description: Width of the container. Can be a pixel number or CSS string. If not set, width is calculated from content.

props.name: containerPadding
props.description: Padding inside the container in pixels.

props.name: rowClass
props.description: CSS class applied to each row wrapper div.

props.name: rowRole
props.description: ARIA role for each row item (e.g.,

props.name: fallback
props.description: Content to display when items array is empty.

props.name: loading
props.description: Loading indicator displayed at the end of the list. Useful for infinite scroll implementations.

props.name: setContainerRef
props.description: Callback to receive the container DOM element reference.

props.name: setScrollPosition
props.description: Callback fired on scroll with current scroll position. Useful for scroll position persistence.

props.name: setAverageRowHeight
props.description: Callback fired when average row height is recalculated. Useful for persisting estimated height for future renders.

props.name: ref
props.description: Imperative handle ref providing scrollToIndex method for programmatic scrolling.

props.name: id
props.description: HTML id attribute for the container.

props.name: role
props.description: ARIA role for accessibility (e.g.,

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible label for the list.

props.name: aria-multiselectable
props.description: Indicates if multiple items can be selected.

props.name: aria-activedescendant
props.description: ID of the currently active/focused item for accessibility.

props.name: onKeyDown
props.description: Keyboard event handler. Called before built-in navigation. Use e.preventDefault() to override.

examples.title: Basic Usage with Variable Heights
examples.description: Items with different content lengths are rendered with their natural heights.

examples.title: Chat Messages
examples.description: Perfect for chat interfaces where messages vary in length.

examples.title: With Selection and Accessibility
examples.description: Accessible listbox with variable height options.

examples.title: Scroll To Index
examples.description: Programmatically scroll to items. Scroll positions are calculated using actual measured heights.

examples.title: Track Average Row Height
examples.description: Monitor the calculated average height for better initial estimates in future renders.

examples.title: Expandable Items
examples.description: Items can change height dynamically. The list automatically adjusts.

examples.title: Empty State
examples.description: Display a fallback when there are no items.

components.name: VirtualList
components.description: Virtualized list that renders only visible items for large datasets. Requires fixed row heights. Supports keyboard navigation, imperative scroll control, and accessibility.

props.name: items
props.description: Reactive accessor returning the array of items to render. Must be a function/accessor, not a raw array.

props.name: rootHeight
props.description: Maximum visible height of the list container in pixels. Determines how many items are visible at once.

props.name: rowHeight
props.description: Fixed height of each row in pixels. This height is enforced on each row wrapper (with overflow: hidden), ensuring accurate scroll calculations. Content exceeding this height will be clipped.

props.name: children
props.description: Render function for each item. Receives the item and a reactive index accessor.

props.name: overscanCount
props.description: Number of extra items to render above and below the visible area. Helps prevent flickering during fast scrolling.

props.name: containerWidth
props.description: Width of the container. Can be a pixel number or CSS string. If not set, width is calculated from content.

props.name: containerPadding
props.description: Padding inside the container in pixels.

props.name: rowClass
props.description: CSS class applied to each row wrapper div.

props.name: fallback
props.description: Content to display when items array is empty.

props.name: loading
props.description: Loading indicator displayed at the end of the list. Useful for infinite scroll implementations.

props.name: setContainerRef
props.description: Callback to receive the container DOM element reference.

props.name: setScrollPosition
props.description: Callback fired on scroll with current scroll position. Useful for scroll position persistence.

props.name: ref
props.description: Imperative handle ref providing scrollToIndex method for programmatic scrolling.

props.name: id
props.description: HTML id attribute for the container.

props.name: role
props.description: ARIA role for accessibility (e.g.,

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible label for the list.

props.name: aria-multiselectable
props.description: Indicates if multiple items can be selected.

props.name: aria-activedescendant
props.description: ID of the currently active/focused item for accessibility.

props.name: onKeyDown
props.description: Keyboard event handler. Called before built-in navigation. Use e.preventDefault() to override.

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Simple virtualized list rendering 1000 items efficiently.

examples.title: With Selection
examples.description: Selectable list with keyboard navigation. Use arrow keys, Home, End, PageUp, PageDown to navigate.

examples.title: Custom Row Styling
examples.description: Apply custom classes to row wrappers using rowClass.

examples.title: Scroll Position Persistence
examples.description: Track scroll position to restore it later (e.g., after navigation).

hooks.name: useCustomResource
hooks.description: A powerful data fetching hook for SolidJS that provides advanced features like automatic retries with exponential backoff, request deduplication, SWR (stale-while-revalidate) caching, IndexedDB persistence, and comprehensive loading/error states. It integrates with CustomResourceProvider for global configuration while allowing per-resource overrides.

parameters.name: urlString
parameters.description: Reactive accessor returning the URL to fetch. Changes trigger a new fetch.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Per-resource configuration that overrides context defaults. See ResourceOptions interface.

parameters.name: refreshKey
parameters.description: Key used to identify this resource for manual refresh via the context refreshData signal.

parameters.name: condition
parameters.description: Reactive condition that must be true for the fetch to execute. Useful for dependent queries.

parameters.name: forceRefresh
parameters.description: When true, bypasses cache and forces a fresh fetch from the server.

parameters.name: pullFromCache
parameters.description: Whether to check IndexedDB cache for previously fetched data.

parameters.name: swr
parameters.description: Enable stale-while-revalidate pattern. Shows cached data immediately while fetching fresh data in background.

returns.name: data
returns.description: The fetched data, or undefined if not yet loaded.

returns.name: error
returns.description: Error object if fetch failed, null otherwise.

returns.name: errorStatus
returns.description: HTTP status code of the error response.

returns.name: loading
returns.description: True during initial load (false with SWR when cached data exists).

returns.name: validating
returns.description: True when revalidating data in background.

returns.name: latest
returns.description: Latest successful data (persists even when refetching).

returns.name: fromCache
returns.description: True if current data came from IndexedDB cache.

returns.name: attempts
returns.description: Current retry attempt count.

returns.name: refetch

returns.name: setErrorStatus
returns.description: Function to manually set the error status.

hooks.name: useDynamicVirtualList
hooks.description: A hook for implementing list virtualization with variable row heights. Unlike useVirtualList which requires fixed heights, this hook measures each row

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: Reactive accessor returning the array of items to virtualize.

parameters.name: rootHeight
parameters.description: The visible height of the container in pixels.

parameters.name: estimatedRowHeight
parameters.description: Initial estimate for row height. Used before actual measurements. Once items are measured, the average measured height is used instead.

parameters.name: overscanCount
parameters.description: Number of extra items to render above and below visible area to reduce flicker.

parameters.name: setScrollPosition
parameters.description: Optional callback to track scroll position changes.

parameters.name: setAverageRowHeight
parameters.description: Optional callback fired when average row height is recalculated. Useful for persisting better estimates.

hooks.returnType: [Accessor<DynamicVirtualListResult<T>>, (e: Event) => void, (index: Accessor<number>, el: HTMLElement) => void, (index: number, behavior?: ScrollBehavior) => { scrollTop: number; behavior: ScrollBehavior }]

returns.name: virtual
returns.description: Reactive accessor containing virtualization state: containerHeight, viewerTop, visibleItems, startIndex, endIndex, and totalItems.

returns.name: handleScroll
returns.description: Scroll event handler to attach to the scrollable container. Updates internal scroll position.

returns.name: registerSize
returns.description: Callback to register a row element for height measurement. Pass to ref of each row element.

returns.name: scrollToIndex
returns.type: (index: number, behavior?: ScrollBehavior) => { scrollTop: number; behavior: ScrollBehavior }
returns.description: Returns scroll parameters for programmatic scrolling. Uses actual measured heights when available.

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Create a custom virtualized list with variable row heights.

examples.title: Understanding registerSize
examples.description: The registerSize function is crucial - it must be called for each row to measure heights.

examples.title: Understanding DynamicVirtualListResult
examples.description: The virtual accessor returns detailed state about visible items.

examples.title: Programmatic Scrolling
examples.description: Scroll to specific items using calculated positions.

examples.title: Tracking Average Height
examples.description: Get the calculated average height for better future estimates.

examples.title: How Height Calculation Works
examples.description: Understanding the internal height management.

examples.title: Cleanup and Memory Management
examples.description: The hook handles cleanup automatically.

examples.title: Types Reference
examples.description: TypeScript types used by useDynamicVirtualList.

examples.title: Comparison with useVirtualList
examples.description: When to use each hook.

hooks.name: useFloating
hooks.description: A positioning engine hook for floating UI elements like tooltips, popovers, and dropdowns. Automatically handles positioning relative to a reference element, viewport boundary detection, placement flipping when there

parameters.name: placement
parameters.description: Initial placement of the floating element. Options:

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Distance in pixels between the reference and floating element.

parameters.name: isOpen
parameters.description: Reactive accessor that controls when positioning calculations run. Position is only computed when isOpen returns true.

parameters.name: renderArrow
parameters.description: Whether to calculate arrow positioning. When true, arrow styles will be computed.

parameters.name: arrowAlignment
parameters.description: Alignment of the arrow along the edge of the floating element.

parameters.name: arrowOffset
parameters.description: Additional offset for the arrow position along the edge of the floating element in pixels.

parameters.name: arrowInset
parameters.description: Controls how far the arrow is inset into the floating element, perpendicular to its edge. Useful for aligning the arrow with borders.

parameters.name: backgroundScrollBehavior
parameters.description: How to handle background scroll when floating is open.

parameters.name: onClose
parameters.description: Callback fired when the floating element should close. Required for

returns.name: floatingStyles
returns.description: Reactive accessor returning CSS styles (position, top, left) to apply to the floating element.

returns.name: arrowStyles
returns.description: Reactive accessor returning CSS styles for the arrow element. Returns null if renderArrow is false.

returns.name: placement
returns.description: Reactive accessor returning the current placement. May differ from initial placement if the element was flipped due to viewport constraints.

returns.name: isAnchorVisible
returns.description: Reactive accessor indicating whether the anchor element is visible within its scrollable ancestors. Useful with backgroundScrollBehavior:

returns.name: update
returns.description: Manually trigger a position recalculation. Useful after content changes.

returns.name: container
returns.description: Reactive accessor returning the container element where the floating element should be portaled.

returns.name: refs.setReference
returns.description: Ref callback to set the reference (trigger) element.

returns.name: refs.setFloating
returns.description: Ref callback to set the floating element.

returns.name: refs.setArrow
returns.description: Ref callback to set the arrow element.

returns.name: refs.reference
returns.description: Accessor to get the reference element.

returns.name: refs.floating
returns.description: Accessor to get the floating element.

returns.name: refs.arrow
returns.description: Accessor to get the arrow element.

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Create a simple floating element that positions below a trigger.

examples.title: With Arrow
examples.description: Add an arrow pointing to the reference element.

examples.title: Placement Options
examples.description: All available placement values.
examples.code:
// Primary placements
            placement: 'top'      // Centered above
            placement: 'bottom'   // Centered below
            placement: 'left'     // Centered to the left
            placement: 'right'    // Centered to the right

            // With alignment variations
            placement: 'top-start'    // Above, aligned to start
            placement: 'top-end'      // Above, aligned to end
            placement: 'bottom-start' // Below, aligned to start
            placement: 'bottom-end'   // Below, aligned to end
            placement: 'left-start'   // Left, aligned to start
            placement: 'left-end'     // Left, aligned to end
            placement: 'right-start'  // Right, aligned to start
            placement: 'right-end'    // Right, aligned to end

            // The hook automatically flips placement if there's
            // not enough space in the preferred direction

examples.title: Reactive Placement
examples.description: The placement accessor returns the actual placement after any flipping.

examples.title: Manual Update
examples.description: Trigger a manual position update when content changes.

examples.title: Scroll Behavior
examples.description: Control how the floating element behaves when the user scrolls.

examples.title: Types Reference
examples.description: TypeScript types used by useFloating.

hooks.name: useIntl
hooks.description: A hook to access the FormatJS internationalization API. Provides methods for formatting messages, dates, numbers, relative times, and lists according to the current locale. Must be used within an IntlProvider.

examples.title: Provider Setup
examples.description: Wrap your application with IntlProvider to enable internationalization.

examples.title: Dynamic Locale Switching
examples.description: Change locale at runtime by updating the locale and messages props.

hooks.name: useMutation
hooks.description: A data mutation hook for handling async operations like POST, PUT, and DELETE requests. Provides reactive state management with loading indicators, error handling, success/error callbacks at both hook and trigger levels, and built-in URL templating for dynamic endpoints.

parameters.name: urlString
parameters.description: The endpoint URL. Can be a static string or reactive accessor. Supports {param} placeholders for URL templating.

parameters.name: options.fetcher
parameters.description: The function that performs the actual mutation. Receives the resolved URL and the argument passed to trigger().

parameters.name: options.onSuccess
parameters.description: Callback fired after successful mutation. Receives the returned data and the original argument.

parameters.name: options.onError
parameters.description: Callback fired when mutation fails. Receives the error and the original argument.

returns.name: data
returns.description: Reactive accessor returning the last successful mutation result. Undefined until first successful mutation.

returns.name: error
returns.description: Reactive accessor returning the last mutation error. Cleared when a new mutation starts.

returns.name: isMutating
returns.description: Reactive accessor indicating if a mutation is in progress. Use for loading states.

returns.name: trigger
returns.type: (arg: TArg, urlArgs?: Record<string, string>, options?: TriggerOptions) => Promise<TData>
returns.description: Function to execute the mutation. Returns a Promise that resolves with the data or rejects with the error.

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Simple mutation for creating a resource.

examples.title: URL Templating
examples.description: Dynamic URLs with parameter substitution.

examples.title: Trigger-Level Callbacks
examples.description: Override or supplement hook-level callbacks per invocation.

examples.title: With Custom Error Type
examples.description: Type your API error responses for full type safety.

examples.title: Reactive URL
examples.description: URL that updates based on reactive state.

examples.title: With Button Loading State
examples.description: Integrate with Button component for consistent UX.

examples.title: Async/Await Pattern
examples.description: Use trigger with try/catch for sequential operations.

examples.title: Types Reference
examples.description: TypeScript types used by useMutation.

hooks.name: useToast
hooks.description: A hook for displaying toast notifications with custom components, auto-dismiss, pause-on-hover, and stacking.

returns.name: [method]
returns.description: Dynamic methods for each registered toast type. Returns toast ID or null.

returns.name: dismiss
returns.description: Dismisses a toast by ID with exit animation.

returns.name: pause
returns.description: Pauses the auto-dismiss timer.

returns.name: play
returns.description: Resumes a paused timer.

examples.title: Setup with ToastProvider
examples.description: Wrap your app and define toast components.

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Show toasts after async operations.

examples.title: Custom Duration
examples.description: Override duration per toast.

examples.title: Pause and Resume
examples.description: Manual timer control.

examples.title: Toast with Progress Bar
examples.description: Visual countdown indicator.

examples.title: Toast with Action Button
examples.description: Add clickable actions.

examples.title: Disable Pause on Hover
examples.description: Always auto-dismiss.

examples.title: ToastProvider Configuration
examples.description: All available props.
examples.code:
<ToastProvider
              // Required: Your toast components
              methods={{
                success: SuccessToast,
                error: ErrorToast,
                warning: WarningToast,
                info: InfoToast,
                loading: LoadingToast,
              }}

              // Position: where toasts appear
              // 'top-left' | 'top-center' | 'top-right' |
              // 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-center' | 'bottom-right'
              position="top-right"

              // Default auto-dismiss time in ms (0 = no auto-dismiss)
              duration={5000}

              // Pause timer when mouse hovers over toast
              pauseOnHover={true}

              // Space between stacked toasts in px
              gutter={16}

              // Maximum visible toasts (oldest dismissed when exceeded)
              maxToasts={5}

              // CSS z-index for toast container
              zIndex={9999}
            >
              {props.children}
            </ToastProvider>

examples.description: Props for custom toast components.

examples.description: Full type definitions.

examples.description: Validation and edge cases.

examples.description: Built-in ARIA support.

import { useToast } from '@kayou/hooks';
        import { ToastProvider } from '@kayou/hooks';

hooks.name: useVirtualList
hooks.description: A hook for implementing list virtualization with fixed row heights. Calculates which items should be visible based on scroll position and returns the necessary data for rendering only the visible portion of a large list. When using this hook directly, you must enforce the rowHeight on each rendered row to ensure accurate scroll calculations. For variable heights, use useDynamicVirtualList instead.

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: Reactive accessor returning the array of items to virtualize.

parameters.name: rootHeight
parameters.description: The visible height of the container in pixels.

parameters.name: rowHeight
parameters.description: Fixed height of each row in pixels. You must enforce this height on each rendered row (e.g., style={{ height: rowHeight +

parameters.name: overscanCount
parameters.description: Number of extra items to render above and below visible area to reduce flicker during scrolling.

parameters.name: setScrollPosition
parameters.description: Optional callback to track scroll position changes.

hooks.returnType: [Accessor<VirtualListResult<T>>, (e: Event) => void, (index: number, behavior?: ScrollBehavior) => { scrollTop: number; behavior: ScrollBehavior }]

returns.name: virtual
returns.description: Reactive accessor containing virtualization state: containerHeight, viewerTop, visibleItems, startIndex, and totalItems.

returns.name: handleScroll
returns.description: Scroll event handler to attach to the scrollable container. Updates internal scroll position.

returns.name: scrollToIndex
returns.type: (index: number, behavior?: ScrollBehavior) => { scrollTop: number; behavior: ScrollBehavior }
returns.description: Returns scroll parameters for programmatic scrolling to a specific index. Does not perform the scroll - use returned values with element.scrollTo().

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Create a custom virtualized list with the hook.

examples.title: Understanding VirtualListResult
examples.description: The virtual accessor returns an object with these properties.

examples.title: Programmatic Scrolling
examples.description: Use scrollToIndex to calculate scroll position for a specific item.

examples.title: Tracking Scroll Position
examples.description: Use setScrollPosition to persist or react to scroll changes.

examples.title: Enforcing Row Height
examples.description: You must enforce the rowHeight on each rendered row to ensure accurate scroll calculations.

examples.title: Types Reference
examples.description: TypeScript types used by useVirtualList.
